publications.title: HCSN: A Hierarchical Causal Structure Network Framework for Emergent Physics

repositories.name: test-hcsn-sim
repositories.description: Simulation engine and experiments for HCSN universes

repositories.name: test-hcsn-theory
repositories.description: Canonical theoretical documents for the HCSN framework

theory_summary.tagline: A causal theory of emergent physics from discrete hypergraph rewrites
theory_summary.core_claim: The universe is made only of causal relations locally rewritten by simple rules. All spacetime, particles, mass, quantum behavior, and relativity emerge from this substrate — nothing is assumed.
what_is_not_assumed: Mass, energy, or momentum as primitives
what_emerges: Quantum behavior (causal ambiguity + finite info resolution)

current_architecture.evolution: Local probabilistic rewrite rules on finite subgraphs
current_architecture.transport_variable: ξ — propagation activity (diagnostic scalar, not a field)

phase_structure.supercritical: Ω > 1.2 — persistent worldlines, stable ξ transport

particle_definition.mass: m ~ 1 / Var(p) where p = rewrite imbalance persistence
particle_definition.interaction: Rewrite competition for Ω-modulated rewrite pool

validated_results: Zero causality violations across all simulation variants
Finite emergent signal speed (~0.086–0.123 normalized units)
Persistent defect worldlines above critical Ω
Uncertainty relation robust across 21+ parameter configurations
Multiverse universality confirmed across 5 rule variants
open_problems: Born rule derivation from causal ignorance
Connection to external large-scale physics frameworks
